exercises.prompt: The first rule of arguing a scientific controversy:

options.text: separate the fact (science's) from the value (society's)
trust whoever has the most diplomas
avoid taking any position

exercises.explanation: « C'est possible » has never meant « c'est souhaitable » — don't let anyone smuggle one into the other.

options.text: when in doubt about grave risk, abstain — it's in the environmental charter
all new technology must be banned
science needs no oversight

exercises.explanation: Dans le doute, s'abstenir — constitutional rank since 2005.

exercises.template: Le comité n'est pas un frein, c'est un {{b1}}.
exercises.explanation: Un garde-fou — a railing/safeguard. The wardrobe and the pantry guard other things.

exercises.prompt: What every technique risks sliding into.
exercises.template: Toute technique a sa {{b1}} ; nommez-la d'avance.
exercises.explanation: Une dérive — the drift toward misuse. La dérivée belongs to your maths class.

options.text: three finalités one technique can serve — and the debate lives between them
three unrelated medical specialties
three stages of a clinical trial

exercises.explanation: Same tool, three masters: healing, enhancing, selecting. Name which one is on the table.
Souhaitable — desirable. The gap between the two words is where ethics lives.

exercises.prompt: Match each bioethics term to its meaning.
Put the words in order: "Who benefits, who decides, who pays?"
Listen to the debater. Which controversy move is this?
exercises.transcript: Oui, la technique est sûre — les essais le montrent. Et oui, la question éthique reste entière : ce ne sont pas les mêmes questions.

options.text: The double concession: science to the worried, the worry to the scientists
A refusal to debate
An appeal to authority

exercises.explanation: Concede both sides their truth, THEN argue — the controversy's noblest move.

exercises.prompt: Read this column on the enhanced human and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: La prothèse qui rend la main ne fait débat nulle part : c'est la médecine dans son lit. Mais la même équipe annonce une main qui dépasse la main — plus forte, infatigable. Le fait est acquis ; la valeur, elle, se discute. Soigner devient améliorer sans changer d'outil, et le marché, lui, a déjà choisi sa finalité préférée. Avant d'applaudir ou de s'indigner, posons les trois questions : qui en bénéficiera — le blessé ou le mieux assuré ? qui décide — le comité ou le catalogue ? qui paie — et en quelle monnaie ?
exercises.passageEn: The prosthesis that gives back the hand is debated nowhere: that's medicine in its own bed. But the same team announces a hand that exceeds the hand — stronger, tireless. The fact is established; the value is up for discussion. Healing becomes enhancing without a change of tool, and the market has already chosen its favourite purpose. Before applauding or protesting, ask the three questions: who will benefit — the injured, or the better-insured? who decides — the committee or the catalogue? who pays — and in what currency?

questions.prompt: Where does the column locate the debate's starting line?

options.text: At the slide from soigner to améliorer — same tool, new purpose
At the invention of any prosthesis
There is no debate at all

questions.prompt: « Qui décide — le comité ou le catalogue ? » opposes…

options.text: ethical oversight versus the market deciding by what it sells
two rival ethics committees
doctors versus patients

exercises.prompt: An ethics committee « rend un avis, pas un verdict » means…

options.text: it advises; the law and society still do the deciding
its decisions are legally binding
it never reaches conclusions

exercises.explanation: Un avis — an opinion. Légiférer remains the legislator's job; the committee holds the lantern.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Le fait est à la science ; la valeur est à la société.

options.text: The fact belongs to science; the value belongs to society.
The prosthesis gives back the hand; the debate starts when it gives back more.
Who benefits, who decides, who pays?
bioethics
to legislate
genetic engineering

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.template: La {{b1}} encadre ce qu'on peut faire du vivant.
exercises.explanation: « La bioéthique encadre ce qu'on peut faire du vivant. » — bioethics.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “Who benefits, who decides, who pays?”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Le fait est à la science ; la valeur est à la société.
exercises.targetEn: The fact belongs to science; the value belongs to society.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Qui en bénéficie, qui décide, qui paie ?
exercises.targetEn: Who benefits, who decides, who pays?
